descript.abstract: Depicts structural range improvements that are lines. This will include fences, Stock Driftway/Feedway (handling facility) and distribution pipelines (Range Water System). These improvements are assets tracking expenditures on the ground across the landscape.
descript.purpose: The data was developed to fill a need for an internal and external spatial representation of the linear structural improvements used in the management of the Rangeland resource. The data will be used to create permit maps in addition to field working maps and any other logical uses that the internal and external public may determine this information is helpful to.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

dataqual.logic: Data stewards have been relied on to validate the data quality and accuracy of the linear structural improvements used in the management of the rangeland resource. Fences, Stock Driftway/Feedways and Distribution Pipelines have been identified using various methods through the years like GPS or digitizing. Spatial data comes from the district and is consolidated at the forest level. This will be a consolidated national extent of the data entered at the lowest level of the agency. Data quality is dependent on quality data maintenance by local data stewards.
dataqual.complete: This data is current only as of the date of publication and may change daily. The need for a daily refresh is key to incorporate any and all local changes as they are made and keep the data in EDW fresh.

horizpa.horizpar: Positional accuracy is veryified by visual comparison to source datasets

procstep.procdesc: Data is the result of a national data call where local units were asked to identify and provide spatial and tabular data to represent linear structural improvements like fences and pipelines. Local units are to follow the current GIS Data Dictionaries and load their local data into the appropriate SDE to be collected and merged into a national extent.

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The numeric code, six digits, for the Admin Organizational unit (RRFFDD where R = Region code number, F = Forest code number, and D = District code number). The administrative organization is the Ranger District on which the feature is located.
The name associated with the Admin Organization identified.

The numeric code for the Managing Organizational unit (RRFFDD where R = Region code number, F = Forest code number, and D = District code number). The managing organization is the Ranger District that manages the feature, regardless of the location of the feature.
The name associated with the Managing Organization identified.
Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases. Specifically for improvements.
A number that uniquely identifies the improvement on the Forest.
The name of the feature. The name need not be unique.
The type of feature.

edom.edomvd: Feature is a RANGE WATER SYSTEM.
Feature is a HANDLING FACILITY
Feature is a FENCE

attr.attrdef: The CN for the range water system a distribution pipeline is linked to.
The ID for the range water system a distribution pipeline is linked to.
The Name for the range water system a distribution pipeline is linked to.

attrdomv.udom: Name of the range water system.

attr.attrdef: The category of the feature.

edom.edomvd: Feature category is a DISTRIBUTION PIPELINE within a Range Water System.
Feature category is a Stock Diftway, Feedway, etc. used for trailing livestock.
Feature category is a FENCE

attr.attrdef: Beginning mile post (BMP).
Ending mile post (EMP).
Length of this section.
Fence Segment Control Number. Will be null if the feature is not a fence.
Fence Segment ID. Will be null if the feature is not a fence.
Fence Segment Name. Will be null if the feature is not a fence.
Construction type of the fence

edom.edomvd: Fence is constructed with Barbed Wire with a smooth bottom wire.
Fence is constructed with Barbed Wire using steel posts.
Fence is constructed with Barbed Wire using steel and wood posts.
Fence is constructed with Barbed Wire using wood posts.
Fence is constructed with blocks.
Fence is constructed with bricks.
Fence is constructed with buck and pole.
Fence is constructed with chain link.
Fence is constructed with concrete.
Fence is constructed with earth.
Fence is constructed with earth with stone or concrete riprap.
Fence is an electric fence.
Fence is constructed with fiberglass.
Fence is constructed with jacks and poles.
Fence is constructed with jacks and poles using outriggers.
Fence is constructed with jacklegs and wire.
Fence is constructed with log and block.
Fence is constructed with metal.
Fence is constructed with other materials.
Fence is constructed with plastic.
Fence is constructed with rockjacks and figure fours.
Fence is constructed with rockjacks and steel posts.
Fence is constructed with rubber.
Fence is constructed with stone.
Fence is constructed with stone masonary with stucco.
Fence is constructed with wood.
Fence is a worm fence.
Fence is constructed with woven wire and a top barbed wire.
Fence is constructed with woven wire.
Fence is constructed with woven wire and is a let down fence.
Fence is constructed with woven wire and a top smooth wire.
Feature is not a fence and as a result does not have a construction type.

attr.attrdef: Fence Segment is a GAP.

edom.edomvd: The segment of fence is a gap where natural features provide the barrier.
The segment of fence is NOT a gap.
Record is not a fence.

attr.attrdef: The year of consttuction.
The year the feature was reconstructed.
The date the feature was last inspected.
Condition and state of repair of the feature.

edom.edomvd: Feature is in EXCELLENT condition.
Feature is in GOOD condition.
Feature is in SATISFACTORY condition.
Feature is in POOR condition.
Feature is in CRITICAL condition.
Feature should be or has been removed.

attr.attrdef: Appraisal rating for the feature.

edom.edomvd: Maintain the feature as is, no major work is planned.
Feature should be removed or eliminated.
Feature is in need of repair.
Feature needs to be replaced or rebuilt.
Feature need to be retrofitted to continue to be useful.
Feature should be relocated to a more effective location.
Features that have been disposed of or no longer exist.

attr.attrdef: Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases.
An alpha numeric identifier for the permit within the Admin Org.
Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases.
An alpha numeric identifier for the permittee.

Revision date, the date and time when the feature was created or last updated. Time is entered using a 24-hour clock (military time) based on the Pacific Standard Time (PST) zone. Tasks associated with each revision should be documented in the process steps of theme-based metadata. Entering the date only is acceptable.
Feature data source code that describes how the data feature was derived or collected.
Distance (+/-), in dataset distance units, of the expected difference between feature and real-world object locations.
A type of UUID (Universal Unique Identifier) in which values are automatically assigned by the geodatabase when a row is created.

attrdomv.udom: A type of UUID (Universal Unique Identifier) in which values are automatically assigned by the geodatabase when a row is created.

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.
Esri field of boundary length in coordinate system units

attrdomv.udom: Esri field of boundary length in coordinate system units

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
